hook_3s: Those 'soft realistic' wellness products? They might be hiding a dirty secret.
script_voiceover: Those 'soft realistic' wellness products? They might be hiding a dirty secret. Many are made from porous materials like TPR, TPE, or PVC, designed to mimic certain textures. But here's the truth: these materials are not truly body-safe. They can harbor bacteria, degrade over time, and even leach harmful chemicals into your body. This isn't about judgment; it's about informed choices for your intimate wellness. Learn more about truly body-safe materials and elevate your intimate wellness journey. Follow us for education you can trust. Always prioritize pure, medical-grade silicone for peace of mind.
b_roll_shots: Gentle hands slowly unwrapping a soft, luxurious fabric.
Close-up of hands gently touching a smooth, non-porous material (e.g., a silicone coaster or smooth stone).
A slow pan over different textures of fabric, some appearing more porous than others.
Close-up of a single flickering candle in a delicate glass holder.
Hands carefully cleaning a smooth, simple object with a soft cloth.
Close-up of hands holding a small, elegant bottle of body-safe lubricant (label not visible).
A serene shot of hands resting peacefully on a soft blanket.
cta: Learn more about truly body-safe materials and elevate your intimate wellness journey. Follow us for education you can trust.
caption: Don't fall for marketing hype. Understanding what your wellness products are made of is crucial for your health and peace of mind. #AIGC
topic_seed: What's actually in 'soft realistic' products (it's not good)
